Engineering notes
What this datasheet is showing and what it is not.

1. This HTML is a complete interactive datasheet, but it is not a raw CatBoost evaluator running in-browser. Instead, the CatBoost models were evaluated offline on structured PVT grids for selected geometry presets, then embedded here for fast local exploration.

2. The geometric design variables are represented as curated presets rather than free sliders. That keeps the file responsive while preserving model fidelity where it matters most operationally: corner, AVDD, temperature, and VCNT.

3. The provided phase-noise models are at 1M, 10M, and 100M offsets. This datasheet therefore shows a three-point phase-noise curve rather than a continuous close-in phase-noise spectrum.

Frequency vs VCNT

AVDD vs VCNT at current preset, corner, and temperature.
Sensitivity snapshot
Finite-difference sensitivities at the current point.

Three-point phase-noise curve on a logarithmic offset axis using the uploaded 1M, 10M, and 100M model outputs.
Phase noise @ 1M vs VCNT
Current preset / selected corner / interpolated at chosen AVDD and temperature.
PVT corner comparison
Frequency and phase-noise spread across process corners at the current AVDD / temperature / VCNT.
Tradeoff cloud
Sampled design-space points from the uploaded model data. Highlight shows current point.

User guide
How to use this AID AI Datasheet™.

1. Start with the preset. Choose a geometry preset first. That selects one transistor/bias design point family.

2. Use the left-side controls to sweep corner, AVDD, temperature, and VCNT. The KPI cards update immediately.

3. Use the Charts tab to understand tuning range, power cost, current draw, CCO node movement, and sensitivity.

4. Use the Phase Noise tab to inspect the three provided phase-noise offsets, compare corners, and review the broader design-space tradeoff cloud.

5. Use AI Query to solve inverse questions such as “What operating point gets me 2.3 GHz under 9 mW with the best phase noise?”

6. Read the notes carefully. The uploaded phase-noise files are at 1M / 10M / 100M. This datasheet therefore does not claim close-in phase noise below 1M offset.

Demo use only. This file is derived from the uploaded VCO surrogate-model artifacts and is intended for engineering exploration, not as a substitute for final signoff simulation or silicon characterization.
